shorts.hook: We raised our boys on commitments not rules
shorts.content: We raised our boys on commitments instead of rules, and it changed everything about how our family operated. When you create commitments, everyone has buy-in. They're part of creating it, so they own it.
shorts.title: This parenting approach changed everything for us

shorts.hook: Your parenting skills ARE business skills
shorts.content: The transition from homeschooling to entrepreneurship requires recognizing that your parenting experience has developed valuable business skills. You've been managing, coaching, and leading this whole time.
shorts.title: Why moms make the best business leaders

shorts.hook: Creating culture at home builds leaders
shorts.content: Creating intentional culture at home through language, agreements, and respect directly translates to better leadership skills in business. The way you lead your family shapes how you'll lead your team.
shorts.title: How family culture creates business leaders

shorts.hook: Single moms raise the strongest leaders
shorts.content: Single mothers can raise confident leaders by modeling resilience and teaching children to take ownership of their choices. You're showing them what strength looks like every single day.
shorts.title: Why single moms raise incredible leaders

titleOptions.curiosityGap: What Happens When You Raise Kids on Commitments Not Rules?
titleOptions.boldStatement: Why Homeschooling Moms Make the Best Executive Coaches
titleOptions.numberBased: 5 Ways Parenting Experience Builds Executive Leadership Skills

description:
Executive coach Amanda Jaggard reveals how raising her sons on commitments instead of rules transformed both her family and her business leadership approach.
Discover why your parenting experience contains the exact skills needed to excel in executive coaching and business leadership.

⏰ TIMESTAMPS:
0:00 Introduction to Amanda Jaggard
2:15 From homeschooling to executive coaching
5:30 Commitments vs rules in family leadership
8:45 Creating intentional culture at home
12:20 Translating parenting skills to business
15:10 Building a coaching practice as a mom
18:30 Preparing for empty nest season
22:00 Single mothers raising confident leaders
25:15 Executive coaching principles for parents

🔑 KEY TAKEAWAYS:
• Commitments create stronger foundations than rules through personal ownership
• Intentional family culture directly translates to better business leadership
• Parenting experience develops valuable entrepreneurial skills
• Executive coaching principles empower both families and businesses
• Empty nest preparation strengthens family relationships

📖 FULL SHOW NOTES: https://themanageher.com/blog/executive-coaching-motherhood-amanda-jaggard-on-culture-commitments-and-raising-leaders

📊 TAKE THE QUIZ: https://themanageher.com/blog/executive-coaching-motherhood-amanda-jaggard-on-culture-commitments-and-raising-leaders

🎧 SUBSCRIBE to The Manage Her® Podcast for weekly leadership insights: https://www.www.youtube.com/@TheManageHer

#ExecutiveCoaching #WomenLeaders #Homeschooling #ParentingTips #LeadershipSkills #MomEntrepreneur #BusinessLeadership #FamilyCulture #RaisingLeaders #WomenInBusiness

communityPosts.content:
Which approach works better in your family? 🏠

A) Traditional rules and consequences
B) Collaborative commitments and agreements
C) A mix of both depending on the situation
D) Still figuring it out!

Amanda Jaggard shared in episode 32 how switching from rules to commitments transformed her family dynamics AND her leadership skills. The results might surprise you! 👇

What's your experience? Drop a comment below!

"We raised our boys on commitments instead of rules, and it changed everything about how our family operated." - Amanda Jaggard 💪

This powerful insight from episode 32 got me thinking... How many leadership skills are we actually developing as parents without even realizing it? 🤔

From managing schedules to coaching through challenges, from creating culture to handling conflicts - sound familiar to anyone running a business or team?

What parenting moment made you realize you were already a leader? Share below! 👇
